response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems software developer with a strong background in web technologies, low-latency services, and developer tooling, but his profile has essentially zero overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, AI accelerator architecture, SIMD/MIMD pipelines, and GPU workload analysis — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His performance work at Sociomantic involved software-level I/O and caching optimizations, not silicon or firmware-level performance modeling. His education is a CS bachelor's degree, which falls short of the preferred PhD/Master's trajectory for this principal-level hardware role.
strengths: Demonstrated ability to build and optimize high-throughput, low-latency software systems (millions of HTTP requests/second at Sociomantic)
Broad polyglot engineering background spanning C/C++, DLang, TypeScript, and embedded contexts, showing adaptability across system levels
Active open-source contributor and community educator, indicating strong technical communication skills
concerns: No SoC, IP modeling, or any chip/silicon design experience whatsoever — the core technical requirement of the role
No background in AI/ML workloads, deep neural network optimization, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD parallelism
Educational background (BS in CS + BS in Fine Arts) and career trajectory are entirely software/web-focused, with no hardware engineering foundation required for a principal-level IC in this domain

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or GPU architecture knowledge
